heading: Protection against the human immunodeficiency virus
label: 1122 Protection against the human immunodeficiency virus
notes: References in TextSection 377E of the Public Health Service Act, referred to in subsec. (a), is classified to , The Public Health and Welfare.section 274f–5 of Title 42

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Whoever, after testing positive for the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) and receiving actual notice of that fact, knowingly donates or sells, or knowingly attempts to donate or sell, blood, semen, tissues, organs, or other bodily fluids for use by another, except as determined necessary for medical research or testing or in accordance with all applicable guidelines and regulations made by the Secretary of Health and Human Services under section 377E of the Public Health Service Act, shall be fined or imprisoned in accordance with subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Transmission Not Required
children.label: b .—Transmission Not Required
children.text: Transmission of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus does not have to occur for a person to be convicted of a violation of this section.

children.heading: .—Penalty
children.label: c .—Penalty
children.text: Any person convicted of violating the provisions of subsection (a) shall be subject to a fine under this title of not less than $10,000, imprisoned for not less than 1 year nor more than 10 years, or both.
